description_long: This church features a rectangular structure with a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof and white plastered walls. The facade is adorned with arched windows featuring gridded panes, and decorative architectural elements such as cornices and pilasters. The main entrance side displays three large arched doorways beneath three tall arched windows, framed by brickwork accents. The side elevations have a series of evenly spaced rectangular and arched windows, while one end of the building is more simply decorated. The roofline is punctuated by modest parapets and ornamental details, but there is no visible steeple or spire. The overall appearance suggests a historical, possibly Baroque or Colonial architectural influence, with a focus on symmetry and restrained ornamentation.
description: A white-walled church with a terracotta-tiled gable roof, arched windows, and decorative facade details, reflecting a historical architectural style.
description_view_0: Side view showing three arched windows and a simple white wall beneath the terracotta-tiled roof.
description_view_1: End view with a gable roof, three arched windows, and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with more rectangular windows and a small section of arched windows, plus additional architectural detailing.
description_view_3: Front view featuring three large arched doorways, three tall arched windows above, and ornate brickwork and cornice details.
